taxonomy: Lathyroides, also known as Sweet Pea, belongs to the family Fabaceae. This classification is due to its characteristics as a flowering plant with compound leaves and pea-like flowers. The genus Lathyrus is part of the tribe Fabeae, which is further divided into several subtribes. Within the genus Lathyrus, the species Lathyrus odoratus is commonly referred to as Sweet Pea or Lathyroides. The species is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its fragrant flowers and climbing habit.

side_effects: Lathyroides has been associated with a condition known as lathyrism, which can lead to neurolathyrism, a potentially irreversible and debilitating condition characterized by paralysis and muscle wasting. The consumption of large amounts of Lathyroides can cause this condition due to the presence of a toxin called ODAP (oxalyldiaminopropionic acid). Lathyroides can also cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in some individuals. In addition, excessive consumption of Lathyroides may lead to kidney and liver problems. Long-term consumption of Lathyroides can also result in neurological issues, including seizures and tremors.
